---
title: "Newton Interpolation Polynomial"
bibliography: ../../../references.bib
---

← [Numerical Methods](../)

Source inspiration: [@mathewsSite].

## Description

Newton interpolation represents the interpolating polynomial in divided-difference form,

$$
P_n(x)=a_0 + a_1(x-x_0)+a_2(x-x_0)(x-x_1)+\cdots+a_n\prod_{j=0}^{n-1}(x-x_j),
$$

where $a_k=f[x_0,\ldots,x_k]$ are divided differences. For the same nodes and data, Newton and Lagrange forms produce the same polynomial; they differ only in representation and numerical workflow.

The 29 animations below intentionally reuse the same interpolation cases as the Lagrange page, matching the legacy Mathews case ordering.

## Animations

Each animation increases polynomial degree one step at a time for a fixed function and interval.

[Julia source for all cases](lagnewt_animations_all.jl)

### Case 01

![Newton interpolation animation for case 01 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt01.gif)

### Case 02

![Newton interpolation animation for case 02 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt02.gif)

### Case 03

![Newton interpolation animation for case 03 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt03.gif)

### Case 04

![Newton interpolation animation for case 04 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt04.gif)

### Case 05

![Newton interpolation animation for case 05 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt05.gif)

### Case 06

![Newton interpolation animation for case 06 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt06.gif)

### Case 07

![Newton interpolation animation for case 07 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt07.gif)

### Case 08

![Newton interpolation animation for case 08 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt08.gif)

### Case 09

![Newton interpolation animation for case 09 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt09.gif)

### Case 10

![Newton interpolation animation for case 10 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt10.gif)

### Case 11

![Newton interpolation animation for case 11 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt11.gif)

### Case 12

![Newton interpolation animation for case 12 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt12.gif)

### Case 13

![Newton interpolation animation for case 13 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt13.gif)

### Case 14

![Newton interpolation animation for case 14 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt14.gif)

### Case 15

![Newton interpolation animation for case 15 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt15.gif)

### Case 16

![Newton interpolation animation for case 16 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt16.gif)

### Case 17

![Newton interpolation animation for case 17 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt17.gif)

### Case 18

![Newton interpolation animation for case 18 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt18.gif)

### Case 19

![Newton interpolation animation for case 19 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt19.gif)

### Case 20

![Newton interpolation animation for case 20 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt20.gif)

### Case 21

![Newton interpolation animation for case 21 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt21.gif)

### Case 22

![Newton interpolation animation for case 22 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt22.gif)

### Case 23

![Newton interpolation animation for case 23 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt23.gif)

### Case 24

![Newton interpolation animation for case 24 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt24.gif)

### Case 25

![Newton interpolation animation for case 25 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt25.gif)

### Case 26

![Newton interpolation animation for case 26 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt26.gif)

### Case 27

![Newton interpolation animation for case 27 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt27.gif)

### Case 28

![Newton interpolation animation for case 28 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt28.gif)

### Case 29

![Newton interpolation animation for case 29 from the legacy function-and-interval list.](lagnewt29.gif)

## Derivation Notes (Planned)

Short derivations will be added for divided differences, nested evaluation, and incremental updates.
